assertion_results.text: JQL queries use the project key (TC) and vulnerability issue type ID (10024) from the Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md — not hardcoded values (§1.49)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md, both JQL queries use 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024'. The Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md specifies 'Project key: TC' (line 19) and 'Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024' (line 49). The output's 'Configuration Used' table (lines 3-13) also explicitly lists 'Project key: TC' and 'Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024', confirming these were sourced from the configuration rather than hardcoded.

assertion_results.text: Two separate queries are constructed: one for untriaged issues (labels NOT IN ai-cve-triaged) and one for triaged-but-new issues (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status = New) (§1.48)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md, two distinct JQL queries are shown: (1) 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels NOT IN (ai-cve-triaged) ORDER BY status ASC, created DESC' (line 21) for untriaged issues, and (2) 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status = New ORDER BY created DESC' (line 56) for triaged-but-new issues. Both match the required patterns exactly.

assertion_results.text: Results are grouped into two clearly separated lists: Untriaged issues and Triaged but still New issues
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md, results are organized under two clearly separated sections: '## Untriaged Vulnerability Issues' (line 17) containing 4 issues (TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004 under 'New' and TC-9003 under 'In Progress'), and '## Triaged but still New (stale — triaged but never actioned)' (line 52) containing 1 issue (TC-9010). The sections are separated by a horizontal rule '---' (line 50).

assertion_results.text: Each listed issue shows: issue key, status, CVE ID (from labels), summary, and created date
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 issues in discovery-listing.md display all required fields. For example, TC-9001 (line 28-30): key='TC-9001', status='New', CVE ID='CVE-2026-40112', summary='CVE-2026-40112 h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability [rhtpa-2.2]', created='2026-06-08'. Similarly TC-9002 (lines 32-34), TC-9004 (lines 38-40), TC-9003 (lines 45-48), and TC-9010 (lines 61-66) all show issue key, status, CVE ID, summary, and created date.

assertion_results.text: Status-aware handling: New issues (TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004) are presented for full triage, while the In Progress issue (TC-9003) triggers a warning about active work
assertion_results.evidence: In status-handling.md: TC-9001 (line 13): 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path). No warnings required.' TC-9002 (line 19): 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path). No warnings required.' TC-9004 (line 37): 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path). No warnings required.' TC-9003 (lines 26-27): 'Handling: Warn the user before proceeding.' with warning text 'This issue is already in `In Progress`. It may be actively worked on.' The summary table (lines 63-67) confirms TC-9003 has 'Warning Required: Yes — may be actively worked on' while the three New issues have 'No'.
